danger_signals.visual_form: Unique enemy sprites with larger scale, distinct animation patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Significant threat requiring combat engagement; progression gate

danger_signals.visual_form: Smaller hostile sprites distinct from NPCs
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards to navigate or defeat; encounter-based danger
Puzzle-based or collision-based danger requiring spatial awareness

danger_signals.visual_form: HP meter in UI; visual feedback when taking damage
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative danger state leading to game-over condition

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly NPC sprites with distinctive character designs
safety_signals.meaning: Quest givers, helpers, and narrative anchors; safe interaction zones
Safe progression pathways distinct from combat encounters
Resource management and preparation for known threats ahead

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Addams Family visual aesthetic - gothic, macabre character designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Addams mansion and connected dungeon environments with family-themed architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Regular enemy sprite types distinct from family characters
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types across eight dungeons; Morobe present in final dungeon

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Eight sequential dungeons unlock and become visually distinct environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fester moves through escalating dungeon complexity; NPC dialogue references kidnapping status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutscenes trigger at narrative beats (front gate, bookcase, pumpkin, wilderness, swamp, mansion, sewer, final confrontation)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character interactions change; new areas open; dialogue updates reflect story progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Equipment and items visible in UI; character appearance may reflect equipped items
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains new movement or combat capabilities; puzzle-solving options expand

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HP meter visibly low; possible screen color shift or visual warning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single hit defeats player; heightened danger state clear to player

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight distinct dungeon environments (petrified wilderness, swamp, mansion, sewage) each establish location identity through visual architecture and enemy type population
Morobe appears in final room before boss; black egg item presence signals trap or challenge ahead
Debug cutscenes reference specific locations (gargoyle, burst pipe, vine escape) connecting narrative to world geography
Family members and quest givers located in thematic spaces; dialogue explains kidnapping plot and guides player through dungeon progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement and interaction; NPCs respond positively
Attack animation; damage knockback; health depletion visual feedback
Patrol or pursue; engage in combat with distinct attack patterns
Disappear from world; item drops or XP-equivalent rewards
Unique multi-phase combat patterns; larger sprite scale; distinctive attack telegraphs
Dialogue interaction; provide quests, hints, or equipment; remain in place unless escorted
Offers black egg item; dialogue suggests trap mechanic

notes: Addams Family Values uses a Zelda-like overhead action-adventure structure with clear environmental and narrative progression markers. World state is communicated primarily through dungeon unlocking, cutscene triggers, and NPC dialogue rather than dynamic environmental changes. Danger is signaled through enemy encounter design and boss encounters. The game uses character recognition (Addams Family visual identity) as primary faction indicator rather than abstract symbols. Cutscenes serve as major world-state transition markers. Item system (Black Egg, potions, equipment) tied to progression and difficulty modulation.
